arts.ttl: Champions League live blog: Atléti-Real Madrid, Arsenal-PSV
arts.sum: Enjoy live updates from Wednesday's Champions League action as Atlético Madrid take on Real Madrid, Arsenal take on PSV and Aston Villa take on Brugge.

arts.ttl: A defensive bargain, the £115m Premier League flop - and Rodri: Arsenal's new sporting director Andrea Berta's BEST and WORST transfers at Atletico Madrid
arts.sum: Andrea Berta may sound like an unknown name for most Premier League fans, but undeniably, he has gained a reputation when it comes to sealing initially thought-impossible deals.

arts.ttl: Atlético Madrid v Real Madrid: Champions League last 16, second leg – live
arts.sum: Champions League updates, 8pm kick-off (first leg: 1-2) Live scores | Read today’s Football Daily | And mail Scott Both teams make two changes to their starting XI after last week’s first leg at the Bernabéu. Atlético call up Conor Gallagher and Reinildo, Javi Galán and Samuel Lino dropping to the bench. Real recall Jude Bellingham and captain Luka Modrić, with Eduardo Camavinga and Brahim Díaz benched. Atlético Madrid: Oblak, Llorente, Gimenez, Lenglet, Reinildo, Simeone, De Paul, Barrios, Gallagher, Griezmann, Alvarez. Subs: Musso, Azpilicueta, Sorloth, Correa, Lemar, Samuel Dias, Molina, Riquelme, Witsel, Galan, Le Normand, Kostis. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Real Madrid legend Ronaldo Nazario pulls out of race for Brazilian Federation
arts.sum: Real Madrid and Brazil icon Ronaldo Nazario has withdrawn from the race to become the next president of the Brazilian Football Federation (CBF). The Real Valladolid President has said that he lacked the support from the regional federations to go ahead with a bid. Ronaldo had declared a desire to run against incumbent President Ednaldo […] The post Real Madrid legend Ronaldo Nazario pulls out of race for Brazilian Federation appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid handed huge boost in Trent Alexander-Arnold free transfer pursuit
arts.sum: Real Madrid's chances of landing Trent Alexander-Arnold in what could be the biggest free agent transfer of the summer are getting stronger by the day.
